# Pipelinez

> Typed, observable record-processing pipelines for .NET.

Pipelinez is a .NET 8 library for building typed source -> segment -> destination record-processing pipelines inside normal application code. It provides lifecycle management, retries, dead-lettering, flow control, performance snapshots, health/metrics, and transport extensions.

## Packages

- `Pipelinez`: core runtime for typed records, sources, segments, destinations, retry, dead-lettering, flow control, performance, and operational tooling.
- `Pipelinez.Kafka`: Kafka source, destination, dead-letter topic, distributed worker, and partition-aware scaling extensions.
- `Pipelinez.PostgreSql`: PostgreSQL destination and dead-letter write extensions with consumer-owned schema mapping and custom SQL support.

## Install

```bash
dotnet add package Pipelinez
dotnet add package Pipelinez.Kafka
dotnet add package Pipelinez.PostgreSql
```

## Use Pipelinez When

- You are building a .NET background worker or service that processes records through typed steps.
- You want a source -> segment -> destination pipeline model instead of hand-wiring TPL Dataflow and runtime plumbing.
- You need retry, dead-lettering, error policies, backpressure, health, metrics, and correlation around record processing.
- You need Kafka-backed ingestion, Kafka publishing, Kafka dead-lettering, or partition-aware Kafka workers.
- You need to write successful records or dead-letter records to PostgreSQL tables owned by your application.

## Do Not Use Pipelinez When

- You need a full distributed stream-processing platform with a large external cluster runtime.
- You primarily need an enterprise service bus with sagas, durable orchestration, routing conventions, and many broker abstractions.
- You only need low-level TPL Dataflow blocks and want to manage lifecycle, retries, metrics, and error handling yourself.

## Common Package Selection

- In-memory or custom transport pipeline: install `Pipelinez`.
- Kafka source or destination: install `Pipelinez.Kafka`.
- PostgreSQL destination or dead-letter table: install `Pipelinez.PostgreSql`.
- Kafka input with PostgreSQL output: install `Pipelinez.Kafka` and `Pipelinez.PostgreSql`.

## Key Concepts

- `PipelineRecord`: base type for records processed by a pipeline.
- `Pipeline<T>`: builder entry point and runtime object.
- `PipelineSegment<T>`: typed processing step.
- `PipelineContainer<T>`: runtime wrapper that carries record metadata, fault state, retry history, and execution history.
- `PipelineErrorAction`: terminal action returned by error handlers, including skip, stop, rethrow, and dead-letter behavior.

## Minimal In-Memory Example

```csharp
using Pipelinez.Core;
using Pipelinez.Core.Record;

public sealed class OrderRecord : PipelineRecord
{
    public required string Id { get; init; }
}

var pipeline = Pipeline<OrderRecord>.New("orders")
    .WithInMemorySource(new object())
    .WithInMemoryDestination("in-memory")
    .Build();

await pipeline.StartPipelineAsync();
await pipeline.PublishAsync(new OrderRecord { Id = "A-100" });
await pipeline.CompleteAsync();
await pipeline.Completion;
```

## Repository

- GitHub: `https://github.com/KenBerg75/Pipelinez`
- License: MIT
- Target framework: .NET 8
